mn151:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the Squirrel Sanctuary.
mn151:1.3: Then in the evening, Venerable Sāriputta rose from seclusion and went to the *Bhagavā*. Having gone to the *Bhagavā*, he paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side. When Venerable Sāriputta was seated to one side, the *Bhagavā* said this to him:
mn151:10.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:10.2: ‘Have the five hindrances been abandoned by me?’
mn151:10.3: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:10.4: ‘The five hindrances have not been abandoned by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the abandoning of the five hindrances.
mn151:10.5: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:10.6: ‘The five hindrances have been abandoned by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:11.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:11.2: ‘Have the five aggregates of clinging/feeding [*pañcupādānakkhandhā*] been fully understood?’
mn151:11.3: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:11.4: ‘The five aggregates of clinging/feeding [*pañcupādānakkhandhā*] have not been fully understood by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the full understanding of the five aggregates of clinging/feeding [*upādānakkhandhānaṁ*].
mn151:11.5: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:11.6: ‘The five aggregates of clinging/feeding [*pañcupādānakkhandhā*] have been fully understood by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:12.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:12.2: ‘Have the four *satipaṭṭhānas* been developed by me?’
mn151:12.3: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:12.4: ‘The four *satipaṭṭhānas* have not been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the development [*bhāvanā*] of the four *satipaṭṭhānas*.
mn151:12.5: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:12.6: ‘The four *satipaṭṭhānas* have been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:13.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:13.2: ‘Have the four right efforts been developed by me?’
mn151:13.3: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:13.4: ‘The four right efforts have not been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the development [*bhāvanā*] of the four right efforts.
mn151:13.5: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:13.6: ‘The four right efforts have been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:14.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:14.2: ‘Have the four bases for power been developed by me?’
mn151:14.3: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:14.4: ‘The four bases for power have not been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the development [*bhāvanā*] of the four bases for power.
mn151:14.5: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:14.6: ‘The four bases for power have been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:15.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:15.2: ‘Have the five *indriyas* been developed by me?’
mn151:15.3: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:15.4: ‘The five *indriyas* have not been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the development [*bhāvanā*] of the five *indriyas*.
mn151:15.5: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:15.6: ‘The five *indriyas* have been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:16.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:16.2: ‘Have the five *balas* been developed by me?’
mn151:16.3: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:16.4: ‘The five *balas* have not been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the development [*bhāvanā*] of the five *balas*.
mn151:16.5: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:16.6: ‘The five *balas* have been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:17.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:17.2: ‘Have the seven *bojjhaṅgas* been developed by me?’
mn151:17.3: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:17.4: ‘The seven *bojjhaṅgas* have not been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the development [*bhāvanā*] of the seven *bojjhaṅgas*.
mn151:17.5: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:17.6: ‘The seven *bojjhaṅgas* have been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:18.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:18.2: ‘Has the noble eightfold path been developed by me?’
mn151:18.3: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:18.4: ‘The noble eightfold path has not been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the development [*bhāvanā*] of the noble eightfold path.
mn151:18.5: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:18.6: ‘The noble eightfold path has been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:19.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:19.2: ‘Have settling [*samatha*] and *vipassanā* been developed by me?’
mn151:19.3: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:19.4: ‘Settling [*samatha*] and *vipassanā* have not been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the development [*bhāvanā*] of settling [*samatha*] and *vipassanā* [*samathavipassanānaṁ*].
mn151:19.5: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:19.6: ‘Settling [*samatha*] and *vipassanā* have been developed by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:2.1: “Your faculties, Sāriputta, are serene, and your complexion is pure and bright.
mn151:2.2: In which dwelling do you now dwell for the most part, Sāriputta?”
mn151:2.3: “Bhante, I now dwell for the most part in the dwelling in emptiness.”
mn151:2.5: It seems, Sāriputta, that you now dwell for the most part in the dwelling of a great person.
mn151:2.6: For this, Sāriputta, is the dwelling of a great person, namely—
mn151:20.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:20.2: ‘Have true knowledge and liberation been realized by me?’
mn151:20.3: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:20.4: ‘True knowledge and liberation have not been realized by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the realization of true knowledge and liberation.
mn151:20.5: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:20.6: ‘True knowledge and liberation have been realized by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:21.1: For whatever ascetics or brahmins there were in the past, Sāriputta, who purified almsfood, all of them purified almsfood in just this same way, by reviewing and reviewing.
mn151:21.2: And whatever ascetics or brahmins there will be in the future, Sāriputta, who will purify almsfood, all of them will purify almsfood in just this same way, by reviewing and reviewing.
mn151:21.3: And whatever ascetics or brahmins there are now, Sāriputta, who purify almsfood, all of them purify almsfood in just this same way, by reviewing and reviewing.
mn151:21.4: Therefore, Sāriputta, you should train thus: ‘By reviewing and reviewing, we will purify almsfood.’—
mn151:21.5: That is how you should train, Sāriputta.”
mn151:21.7: Venerable Sāriputta was glad and approved of what the *Bhagavā* said.
mn151:21.8: The ninth, Purification of Almsfood, is finished.
mn151:3.1: Therefore, Sāriputta, if a *bhikkhu* should wish:
mn151:3.2: ‘May I dwell for the most part in the dwelling in emptiness,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should reflect thus:
mn151:3.3: ‘On the road by which I entered the village for alms, in whatever place I walked for alms, and on the road by which I returned from the village after alms, is there in me there, with regard to forms knowable by the eye, any desire, lust, ill will, delusion, or even mental resistance?’
mn151:3.4: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:3.5: ‘On the road by which I entered the village for alms, in whatever place I walked for alms, and on the road by which I returned from the village after alms, there is in me there, with regard to forms knowable by the eye, desire, lust, ill will, delusion, or even mental resistance,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the abandoning of those very bad, unskillful *dhammas*.
mn151:3.6: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:3.7: ‘On the road by which I entered the village for alms, in whatever place I walked for alms, and on the road by which I returned from the village after alms, there is not in me there, with regard to forms knowable by the eye, desire, lust, ill will, delusion, or even mental resistance,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:4-8.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:4-8.10: ‘On the road by which I entered the village for alms, in whatever place I walked for alms, and on the road by which I returned from the village after alms, there is not in me there, with regard to *dhammas* knowable by the mind, desire, lust, ill will, delusion, or even mental resistance,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:4-8.2: ‘On the road by which I entered the village for alms, in whatever place I walked for alms, and on the road by which I returned from the village after alms, is there in me there, with regard to sounds knowable by the ear …
mn151:4-8.5: tangible things knowable by the body …
mn151:4-8.6: with regard to *dhammas* knowable by the mind, any desire, lust, ill will, delusion, or even mental resistance?’
mn151:4-8.7: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:4-8.8: ‘On the road by which I entered the village for alms, in whatever place I walked for alms, and on the road by which I returned from the village after alms, there is in me there, with regard to *dhammas* knowable by the mind, desire, lust, ill will, delusion, or even mental resistance,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the abandoning of those very bad, unskillful *dhammas*.
mn151:4-8.9: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:9.1: Again further, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn151:9.2: ‘Have the five strands of sensual pleasure been abandoned by me?’
mn151:9.3: If, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:9.4: ‘The five strands of sensual pleasure have not been abandoned by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should make an effort for the abandoning of the five strands of sensual pleasure.
mn151:9.5: But if, Sāriputta, while reviewing, a *bhikkhu* knows thus:
mn151:9.6: ‘The five strands of sensual pleasure have been abandoned by me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness, training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
